chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Marriage of the Zimmy woman (under the responsibility)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/64)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/64)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ نِكَاحِ الْيَهُودِيَّةِ وَ النَّصْرَانِيَّةِ فَقَالَ لَا يَصْلُحُ لِلْمُسْلِمِ أَنْ يَنْكِحَ يَهُودِيَّةً وَ لَا نَصْرَانِيَّةً وَ إِنَّمَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ مِنْهُنَّ نِكَاحُ الْبُلْهِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washa, from Aban Bin Usman, from Zurara Bin Ayn who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> about marrying the Jewish woman and the Christian woman. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is not correct for the Muslim man that he marries a Jewish woman, nor a Christian woman. But rather, it is Permissible for him from them, marrying the simple ones (not aware of their Religion)’.
en.sarwar: 2. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali al-Washsha’ from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Zurarah ibn ‘A‘yan who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about marriage with a Jewish or Christian woman. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not proper for a Muslim to marry a Jewish or Christian woman; only with the simple-minded ones of them marriage is lawful.’”
